title: Avoid injecting non-singleton beans into singleton beans
description: Injecting non-singleton Spring beans into singleton beans can cause unexpected behavior due to differing bean lifecycles. This may result in stale references or thread-safety issues. Always ensure that beans injected into singletons are also singletons to maintain consistent application state.
recommendation: Refactor your code so that only singleton beans are injected into singleton beans, or use appropriate scopes and proxies if necessary.

checks.message: Refactor your code so that only singleton beans are injected into singleton beans, or use appropriate scopes and proxies if necessary.
